rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees with valid component scope and typed expressions.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Declare auxiliary variables with explicit types matching their expressions.
Avoid placing implementation-level assignments in type-scoped contracts.
Omit clauses that reference AADL properties or opaque data types without scalar representation.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must use valid component feature names and types.
Assignments are only valid in implementation scopes for output features or declared variables.
Boolean expressions must match the actual data type of the referenced port or variable.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
Do not assume Boolean ports can be treated as integers or use 0/1 comparisons.
Do not place output guarantees in implementation-level annexes.
Do not generate range assumptions without visible feature names or concrete bounds.
